rationale: All three review lenses (blind, edge-case, acceptance) ran successfully against the 58-line diff. No patch or decision_needed findings survived triage: 1 finding deferred (regression test's sys.modules-eviction approach doesn't fully represent the standard enterprise-installed CI/dev environment, but it exactly mirrors the codebase's established test_webhook_rate_limiter_import.py convention that spec/plan explicitly directed following, and a real fix is out of scope for this XS ticket), and 2 findings dismissed (speculative get_type_hints()/NameError risk on a plain SDK callback contradicted by 20+ files using the identical pattern in production; a diff-visibility caveat resolved by directly reading the full current file, confirming ExperimentItem is used only as a type annotation at line 134). Acceptance lens's partial marks on RED/GREEN evidence are resolved: this session's Stage 5 TDD execution independently verified RED then GREEN inside the codemie-oss-test Docker container, and confirmed no regressions in the existing test_workflow_evaluation_service.py suite.

business_review.item: workflow_evaluation_service.py imports ExperimentItem only under TYPE_CHECKING
business_review.notes: Confirmed by direct read of the committed file: lines 23-24 wrap the import in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`, paired with existing `from __future__ import annotations`; ExperimentItem used only as a type annotation at line 134.

business_review.item: New regression test fails (RED) against the pre-fix import when langfuse is hidden from sys.modules
business_review.notes: Verified in Stage 5 of this session inside the codemie-oss-test Docker container (temporarily reverted the TYPE_CHECKING guard, ran the new test, observed FAILED/AssertionError, then restored the fix). The diff/spec alone don't show this evidence, but the session transcript is the record per sdlc-standard's design.

business_review.item: New regression test passes (GREEN) against the fixed import
business_review.notes: Verified in the same Docker container immediately after RED, with the TYPE_CHECKING fix restored: test PASSED.

Full suite run in the Docker container this session; all tests PASSED. These tests mock require_langfuse_client at the call site and never exercise the import path, so they are unaffected by the import restructuring.

business_review.item: Diff and commit contain only workflow_evaluation_service.py and the new test file
business_review.notes: code-review.diff contains exactly two hunks (the service file's import change, and the new 34-line test file); commit 7b01414f0 shows 2 files changed. Unrelated dirty files (.env, .codemie/codemie-cli.config.json, google_oauth.py, sharepoint_oauth.py) are excluded.

standards_review.notes: Commit message "EPMCDME-13525: Fix OSS build startup crash from top-level langfuse import" matches the required `EPMCDME-####: Description` format in .ai-run/guides/standards/git-workflow.md.

New test file carries the required Apache 2.0 license header per .ai-run/guides/standards/code-quality.md; import restructuring uses existing TYPE_CHECKING/future-annotations convention already used in 20+ files.

Checked against .ai-run/guides/development/security-patterns.md (authentication/authorization, secret handling). Diff is an import reorganization plus a new test file; touches no auth, secrets, or logging surface. No applicable checklist violations.
